articles.title: Prohibited AI Practices
articles.description: AI practices that are prohibited outright: subliminal manipulation, exploitation of vulnerabilities, social scoring, criminal risk prediction from profiling alone, untargeted facial recognition scraping, workplace/education emotion recognition, and biometric categorisation for protected attributes.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 35 million or 7% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Classification Rules for High-Risk AI Systems
articles.description: Defines two paths to high-risk classification: (1) AI as safety component of Annex I products requiring third-party conformity assessment; (2) AI systems listed in Annex III. Includes Article 6(3) derogation for systems that do not pose significant risk.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Risk Management System
articles.description: High-risk AI systems must have a continuous, iterative risk management system throughout the lifecycle. Must include risk identification, estimation, evaluation, and mitigation. Must function as deployment gates in CI/CD, not advisory documents.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Data and Data Governance
articles.description: Training, validation, and testing datasets must be relevant, sufficiently representative, and as error-free as possible. Requires examination for possible biases, dataset versioning, data lineage, and bias evaluation reports.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Technical Documentation
articles.description: High-risk AI systems must have technical documentation drawn up before placement on the market and kept up to date. Must cover Annex IV requirements across nine structured categories.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Record-Keeping and Logging
articles.description: High-risk AI systems must have automatic logging capabilities that record events relevant to identifying risk situations and substantial modifications throughout the lifecycle. Minimum retention of six months (Article 19).
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers
articles.description: High-risk AI systems must be designed to be sufficiently transparent for deployers to interpret output and use appropriately. Requires model cards, confidence score exposure, system limitation disclosures, and deployer instructions.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Human Oversight
articles.description: High-risk AI systems must be designed to allow effective human oversight during use. Three patterns: human-in-the-loop (review before action), human-on-the-loop (monitoring with intervention), human-over-the-loop (governance). Must include kill-switch capability.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Accuracy, Robustness, and Cybersecurity
articles.description: High-risk AI systems must achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity. Requires evaluation suites, drift detection, adversarial testing, input validation, and rollback capability.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Responsibilities Along the AI Value Chain
articles.description: Any person who substantially modifies a high-risk AI system becomes a new provider. Fine-tuning, adding RAG, or wrapping in agentic orchestration can trigger role transition from deployer to provider.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment
articles.description: Deployers of high-risk AI systems that are bodies governed by public law or private entities providing public services must perform a fundamental rights impact assessment before deployment.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Transparency Obligations for All AI Systems
articles.description: All AI systems interacting with natural persons must disclose they are AI. AI-generated content must be machine-readably marked. Emotion recognition and biometric categorisation systems must inform exposed persons. Deepfakes must be disclosed.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 7.5 million or 1% of worldwide annual turnover

articles.title: Post-Market Monitoring by Providers
articles.description: Providers of high-risk AI systems must establish a post-market monitoring system proportionate to the nature and risks. Must monitor accuracy drift, input/output distribution shift, latency, error rates by category, and human override rates.
articles.penaltyDescription: Up to EUR 15 million or 3% of worldwide annual turnover
